chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-4177 of 2020 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. For orders on M.A. No.17734/2020 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.17735/2020 (Ex/A). 3. For orders on M.A. No.17736/2020 (Stay/A) 4. For hearing of main case -------------------------------------------------------------- 10.09.2020. Mr. Muhammad Nazeer Abbasi, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Kafeel Ahmed Abbasi, DAG. >><< 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 3&4. This petition has been filed on the ground that some discriminatory treatment has been meted out with the petitioner while valuing the goods imported by it. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that he would be satisfied and would not press this petition, if directions may be given to the respondent /Customs Authorities for assessing the value of the goods imported by the petitioner at par with the goods imported by other importers, which are similar in nature, in accordance with law, without giving any discriminatory treatment to the goods imported by the petitioner. Mr. Kafeel Ahmed Abbasi, learned DAG present in Court waives notice and states that this petition could be disposed of by directing the Customs Authorities not to make discriminatory treatment with the petitioner. We have heard both the learned counsel at some length and have also perused the record. We, therefore, under the circumstances, dispose of this petition by directing the Customs Authorities that if goods imported by the petitioner are similar in nature on every respect and aspect with the goods imported by the other importers, there should not be any discriminatory treatment with the goods imported by the petitioner while assessing their value.

We have heard both the learned counsel at some length and have also perused the record. We, therefore, under the circumstances, dispose of this petition by directing the Customs Authorities that if goods imported by the petitioner are similar in nature on every respect and aspect with the goods imported by the other importers, there should not be any discriminatory treatment with the goods imported by the petitioner while assessing their value. -2- With these directions, this petition stands disposed of along with the listed application. JUDGE JUDGE
